title: Remote Ethernet
description:
Remote Ethernet

Remote Ethernet

Remote Ethernet makes it possible to control the robot and controller from external equipment by sending the remote commands through Ethernet (TCP/IP).

NOTE:  For enhanced security, passwords must be set when connecting Controllers and PCs.

See details below.

1.7  Security for Controller Ethernet Connection

1.8  Security for Compact Vision CV2-B Ethernet Connection

1.9  Security for Feeder Ethernet Connection

Remote Ethernet Configuration

To set the remote Ethernet functions valid, follow the procedures below to configure the parameter.

In
	 the Epson RC+ 8.0 menu, select [Setup]-[System Configuration] and
	 display the [System Configuration] dialog box. Select [Controller]-[Remote]-[Ethernet].

Configure
	 the necessary items for the remote Ethernet control.

Click
	 the [Apply] button to save the new setting, then click the [Close]
	 button.

For details on the dialog setting, refer to section 5.13.2 [System Configuration] (Setup Menu) - [Setup]-[System Configuration]-[Controller]-[Remote].

If you select "Use only for monitoring" and only acquire values using Remote Ethernet control, the "Control Device Configuration" in the next section will not be necessary .

Control Device Configuration

Set the control device to "Remote Ethernet" by the following procedure.

In
	 the Epson RC+ 8.0 menu-, select [Setup]-[System Configuration] and
	 display the [System Configuration] dialog box. Select [Controller]-[Configuration].

	 Select "Remote Ethernet" in the [Controller device] box.

Click the [Apply] button to save the new
	 settings, and then click the [Close] button.

For details on the dialog setting, refer to section 5.13.2 [System Configuration] (Setup menu) - [Setup]-[System Configuration]-[Configuration].

Remote Ethernet Control Execution

Set the remote control available by the following procedure.

Connect
	 from client equipment to the specified port in the Remote Ethernet
	 of the Controller.

Specify
	 the password set in the Remote Ethernet to the parameter and send
	 the Login command.

Client
	 equipment has to wait until Auto (GetStatus command response) is ON,
	 before execution of remote command.

Now remote
	 command will be accepted.

	Each command executes the function the input acceptance condition.

Debugging Remote Ethernet Control

Program debug from Epson RC+ 8.0 development environment is capable as follows.

Build
	 a program as usual.

Open
	 the Run window and click the [Enable Remote ETH] button.

When
	 you only acquire values using the Remote Ethernet control, the [Enable
	 Remote ETH] button is not displayed. Click the <Start> button
	 of the device specified as the control device.

Now
	 remote command will be accepted.

Breakpoint setting and output to the Run window is available.

NOTE:  If not Login within 5 minutes from external equipment, the connection will be cut down automatically. After Login, if no command is sent within the timeout duration of the remote Ethernet, connection will be cut down. In this case, establish the connection again.

If error occurs, execute the Reset command to clear the error condition before executing the operation command. To clear the error condition from external equipment by monitoring, use the "GetStatus" and "Reset" command.

CAUTION	■	If you
		 set "0" in the [Timeout] box, time out duration is infinite. In
		 this case, the task continues to execute even without the communication
		 from client. This means the robot may keep moving and cause unexpected
		 damage. Ensure the ways other than the communication to stop the
		 task.
